rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative amino- and oxyselenation of styrenes with diphenyl diselenide and amine or alcohol nucleophiles to give vicinal aminoselenides or oxyselenides under undivided-cell, metal- and oxidant-free conditions
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of PhSeSePh generates PhSe• and PhSe-; anodic oxidation regenerates PhSe• from PhSe- (and/or oxidizes the styrene/benzylic radical to a benzylic cation); a parallel anodic path oxidizes PhSeSePh to a seleniranium-type intermediate that is attacked by NuH

key_intermediates.name: β-(phenylseleno)benzylic radical B (from PhSe• addition to styrene)
β-(phenylseleno)benzylic cation C (anodic oxidation of B)
cyclic seleniranium ion E (from PhSeSePh anodic oxidation)
ammonium/oxocarbenium adduct D (NuH attack on C)

electron_flow: At the cathode, PhSeSePh is reduced to PhSe• and PhSe- (with concomitant H2 evolution from ROH); PhSe• adds regioselectively to the styrene to give a benzylic radical B, which is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation C. Nucleophile (amine or alcohol) attacks C and deprotonation gives the 1,2-difunctionalized product 4; alternatively PhSeSePh is directly oxidized at the anode to a seleniranium ion E that is opened by NuH.

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted styrene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: ring-opened/retained product 8f formed (73%), consistent with a carbon-radical intermediate from PhSe• addition

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) - CV
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak at 1.88 V and reduction peak at -1.74 V; indicates both anodic and cathodic involvement

probe_reagents.name: benzotriazole 5a - CV
probe_reagents.observation: no obvious oxidation peak, ruling out direct anodic oxidation of the N-nucleophile

notes: Two parallel anodic pathways are proposed: (i) anodic oxidation of the β-selenyl benzylic radical B to cation C followed by NuH addition (favored), and (ii) anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to a cyclic seleniranium intermediate E that is opened by NuH. H2 evolution from ROH at the cathode is part of the paired electrolysis. No TEMPO/BHT trapping or KIE was reported.
